qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in Nargo.toml entry field allows reading arbitrary files
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes path traversal via unsanitized Nargo.toml entry field (CWE-22, missing path sanitization in tooling/nargo_toml/src/lib.rs). The advisory describes heap corruption from incorrect buffer size calculation in allocate_foreign_call_result_array() in the Brillig compiler (CWE-131). Different vulnerable code, different root cause, different component, different vulnerability class.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stack overflow in Noir formatter via deeply nested member access chains
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes unbounded recursion in format_member_access_impl() in the Noir formatter (tooling/nargo_fmt). The advisory describes incorrect buffer size calculation in allocate_foreign_call_result_array() in the Brillig compiler's foreign call codegen. Different component (formatter vs Brillig compiler), different root cause (unbounded recursion vs incorrect size computation), different vulnerability class (DoS via stack overflow vs heap corruption via under-allocation).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes heap corruption in allocate_foreign_call_result_array() in the Brillig compiler, where nested_size (semantic element count) is used instead of the correct semi-flattened size from compute_array_length(). Neither qualifying finding involves the Brillig compiler's foreign call result allocation, incorrect buffer size calculation (CWE-131), or heap corruption from under-allocation. The findings describe unrelated vulnerabilities: path traversal in Nargo.toml entry field and stack overflow in the Noir formatter.
evidence: Advisory root cause: allocate_foreign_call_result_array() uses nested_size instead of compute_array_length() result for nested arrays in Brillig foreign call codegen
Advisory affected component: Brillig bytecode compiler, brillig_call/code_gen_call.rs
Advisory CWE: CWE-131 (Incorrect Calculation of Buffer Size)
entry_path_traversal: different component (nargo_toml), different root cause (missing path sanitization), different CWE (CWE-22)

formatter_stack_overflow: different component (nargo_fmt), different root cause (unbounded recursion vs incorrect size computation), different vulnerability class (DoS via stack overflow vs heap corruption)
